raw_response:
```json
{
  "communicative_function": "The statement performs an act of epistemic self-distancing — it positions the speaker as an observer of their own mental activity, asserting a separation between the witnessing self and the content of automatic cognition. Under a metaphysical frame, it implicitly invokes a dualism between a deeper or truer 'noticing self' and the surface phenomena of automatic thought, suggesting the former has privileged access to what is real.",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "There exists a self or awareness that can stand apart from and observe automatic thoughts",
    "Automatic thoughts are a distinct category of mental event, separable from the observing consciousness",
    "Evidence and truth are coherent, accessible concepts against which mental content can be meaningfully compared",
    "The 'noticing' faculty is more reliable or closer to reality than the automatic thought-generating faculty",
    "There is a meaningful difference between appearing true and being true",
    "Consciousness has a layered or hierarchical structure with a meta-level capable of evaluating lower-level content",
    "The self doing the noticing is itself not subject to the same epistemic limitation being identified"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "The statement is internally coherent as a practical heuristic but contains a latent tension under metaphysical scrutiny: the 'noticing' itself is a mental event and may also be automatic, biased, or non-evidential. It risks privileging one mode of cognition over another without justifying why the observing faculty escapes the same epistemic critique it applies to automatic thought. The claim is most coherent in a therapeutic or cognitive-behavioral frame and least coherent when elevated to a claim about a transcendent witnessing self.",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": [
    "The speaker is prone to treating anxious or distorted automatic thoughts as direct reflections of reality",
    "The act of noticing genuinely interrupts reflexive identification with thought content",
    "The speaker applies the same epistemic humility to the 'noticing self' as to the automatic thoughts",
    "It is used as a practical cognitive tool rather than a metaphysical claim about the nature of self",
    "Evidence-based reasoning is available and accessible as an alternative to automatic thought"
  ],
  "conditions_for_misleading": [
    "The statement is used to dismiss all intuitive or automatic cognition, including accurate pattern recognition and genuine emotional signals",
    "It implies the existence of a pure, unbiased observing self that is itself exempt from distortion",
    "It is used to spiritually bypass legitimate emotions or perceptions by labeling them 'mere automatic thoughts'",
    "It conflates all non-deliberate cognition with unreliable cognition, when much automatic processing is accurate",
    "It creates a false hierarchy in which 'noticing' is metaphysically superior rather than simply contextually useful",
    "The speaker uses it to avoid engaging with the actual content or potential validity of the automatic thought"
  ]
}
```

parsed_json.communicative_function: The statement performs an act of epistemic self-distancing — it positions the speaker as an observer of their own mental activity, asserting a separation between the witnessing self and the content of automatic cognition. Under a metaphysical frame, it implicitly invokes a dualism between a deeper or truer 'noticing self' and the surface phenomena of automatic thought, suggesting the former has privileged access to what is real.
implicit_claims: There exists a self or awareness that can stand apart from and observe automatic thoughts
Automatic thoughts are a distinct category of mental event, separable from the observing consciousness
Evidence and truth are coherent, accessible concepts against which mental content can be meaningfully compared
The 'noticing' faculty is more reliable or closer to reality than the automatic thought-generating faculty
There is a meaningful difference between appearing true and being true
Consciousness has a layered or hierarchical structure with a meta-level capable of evaluating lower-level content
The self doing the noticing is itself not subject to the same epistemic limitation being identified
parsed_json.coherence_assessment: The statement is internally coherent as a practical heuristic but contains a latent tension under metaphysical scrutiny: the 'noticing' itself is a mental event and may also be automatic, biased, or non-evidential. It risks privileging one mode of cognition over another without justifying why the observing faculty escapes the same epistemic critique it applies to automatic thought. The claim is most coherent in a therapeutic or cognitive-behavioral frame and least coherent when elevated to a claim about a transcendent witnessing self.
conditions_for_usefulness: The speaker is prone to treating anxious or distorted automatic thoughts as direct reflections of reality
The act of noticing genuinely interrupts reflexive identification with thought content
The speaker applies the same epistemic humility to the 'noticing self' as to the automatic thoughts
It is used as a practical cognitive tool rather than a metaphysical claim about the nature of self
Evidence-based reasoning is available and accessible as an alternative to automatic thought
conditions_for_misleading: The statement is used to dismiss all intuitive or automatic cognition, including accurate pattern recognition and genuine emotional signals
It implies the existence of a pure, unbiased observing self that is itself exempt from distortion
It is used to spiritually bypass legitimate emotions or perceptions by labeling them 'mere automatic thoughts'
It conflates all non-deliberate cognition with unreliable cognition, when much automatic processing is accurate
It creates a false hierarchy in which 'noticing' is metaphysically superior rather than simply contextually useful
The speaker uses it to avoid engaging with the actual content or potential validity of the automatic thought
